segments.text: Lesson 25.

translation.text: 第25课。

segments.text: The Cutty Sark.

translation.text: “卡蒂萨克号”帆船。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What piece of bad luck prevented the Cutty Sark from winning the race?

translation.text: 哪次厄运使“卡蒂萨克号”未能赢得比赛？

segments.text: One of the most famous sailing ships of the 19th century, the Cutty Sark, can still be seen at Greenwich.

translation.text: 19世纪最著名的帆船之一“卡蒂萨克号”，如今仍可在格林尼治看到。

segments.text: She stands on dry land and is visited by thousands of people each year.

translation.text: 它停放在陆地上，每年有成千上万人前来参观。

segments.text: She serves as an impressive reminder of the great ships of the past.

translation.text: 它令人印象深刻地唤起人们对昔日巨轮的记忆。

segments.text: Before they were replaced by steamships, sailing vessels like the Cutty Sark were used to carry tea from China and wool from Australia.

translation.text: 在被轮船取代以前，像“卡蒂萨克号”这样的帆船曾用于从中国运茶叶、从澳大利亚运羊毛。

segments.text: The Cutty Sark was one of the fastest sailing ships that has ever been built.

translation.text: “卡蒂萨克号”是有史以来建造的最快帆船之一。

segments.text: The only other ship to match her was the Thermopylae.

translation.text: 唯一能与它匹敌的是“塞莫皮莱号”。

segments.text: Both these ships set out from Shanghai on June 18, 1872, on an exciting race to England.

translation.text: 1872年6月18日，两艘船同时从上海启航，展开了一场驶往英国的激动人心的比赛。

segments.text: This race, which went on for exactly four months, was the last of its kind.

translation.text: 比赛整整持续了四个月，也是同类比赛中的最后一场。

segments.text: It marked the end of the great tradition of ships with sails and the beginning of a new era.

translation.text: 它标志着大型帆船传统的终结和新时代的开始。

segments.text: The first of the two ships to reach Java after the race had begun was the Thermopylae, but on the Indian Ocean the Cutty Sark took the lead.

translation.text: 比赛开始后，两船中首先抵达爪哇的是“塞莫皮莱号”，但进入印度洋后，“卡蒂萨克号”取得领先。

segments.text: It seemed certain that she would be the first ship home, but during the race she had a lot of bad luck.

translation.text: 看来它肯定会最先回到英国，但比赛中它遭遇了连番厄运。

segments.text: In August she was struck by a very heavy storm during which her rudder was torn away.

translation.text: 8月，它遇上一场猛烈风暴，船舵被卷走。

segments.text: The Cutty Sark rolled from side to side, and it became impossible to steer her.

translation.text: “卡蒂萨克号”左右摇摆，完全无法操纵。

segments.text: A temporary rudder was made on board from spare planks, and it was fitted with great difficulty.

translation.text: 船员用备用木板在船上制成一个临时船舵，并费尽周折将它装好。

segments.text: This greatly reduced the speed of the ship, for there was a danger that if she traveled too quickly, this rudder would be torn away as well.

translation.text: 这大大降低了航速，因为如果开得太快，临时船舵也有被卷走的危险。

segments.text: Because of this, the Cutty Sark lost her lead.

translation.text: 因此，“卡蒂萨克号”失去了领先地位。

segments.text: After crossing the equator, captain called in at a port to have a new rudder fitted, but by now the Thermopylae was over five hundred miles ahead.

translation.text: 越过赤道后，船长驶入一个港口安装新舵，但此时“塞莫皮莱号”已领先500多英里。

segments.text: Though the new rudder was fitted at tremendous speed, it was impossible for the Cutty Sark to win.

translation.text: 尽管新舵以惊人的速度装好，“卡蒂萨克号”仍不可能获胜。

segments.text: She arrived in England a week after the Thermopylae.

translation.text: 它比“塞莫皮莱号”晚一周抵达英国。

segments.text: Even this was remarkable, considering that she had had so many delays.

translation.text: 考虑到途中耽搁如此之多，这个成绩仍十分了不起。

segments.text: There is no doubt that if she had not lost her rudder, she would have won the race easily.

translation.text: 毫无疑问，如果没有失去船舵，它本可以轻松赢得比赛。
